CATHERINE DELCIN'S MOTION OF ATTORNEY FOR AN ORDER TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE ORDER UNDER CAL. CIV. PROC. 473. (ADDED FOR THE POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING]
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Attorney Catherine Delcin For An Order To Vacate And Set Aside Order Under Cal. Civ. Proc. 473; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: CATHERINE DELCIN'S Motion For An Order To Vacate And Set Aside Order Under Cal. Civ. Proc. 473.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tenative ruling. The motion is GRANTED. Based on the materials submitted with the motion, it does not appear that, at the time Pro Tem Deol granted Cross-Complainants' motion for sanctions on June 20, 2024, Pro Tem Deol had been apprised by counsel for Cross-Complainants that Ms. Declin's motion to be relieved as counsel of record for Embattle Protective Services ("Embattle") had been granted on May 15, 2024. It likewise appears, based on the materials submitted with the motion, that Ms. Declin did not have notice of Pro Tem Deol's tentative ruling granting said motion nor the opportunity to be heard to contest it. The notice of motion did state that sanctions would be sought against Ms. Declin in her capacity as Embattle's counsel, but by the time the motion came on for hearing Ms. Declin had not been Embattle's counsel for over a month and so reasonably would not have expected the motion to continue to seek relief as to her personally. While Cross-Complainants contend that Ms. Declin was only relieved as counsel for Embattle in its capacity as a "Defendant," but not in its capacity as a "Cross-Defendant," the materials submitted with the motion do not support that interpretation. Further, it appears that, before her motion to be relieved as counsel of record for Embattle was granted, Ms. Declin acted appropriately to protect her client's interests by serving objections-only discovery responses in a period when her client would not respond to her in order to avoid her client waiving its right to object. Accordingly, good cause exists to vacate and set aside Pro Tem Deol's June 20, 2024 sanctions order under Code of Civil Procedure section 473, subdivision b.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to mkahn@gibsondunn.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JONATHAN MEISLIN, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm, PLC (tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiffs.
No appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EMBATTLE PROTECTIVE SERVICES' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Embattle Protective Services' demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled.

The complaint pleads that Kassandra Gomez and Erica Contreras went to a San Francisco dance club "frequented by dangerous and violent people," where Embattle provided security. (Id. at 5:24.) A male patron "twice attempted to steal Gomez' phone from her purse" and "pushed both Gomez and Contreras." (Id. 4:18-22.) Later, outside the club, the male patron again pushed plaintiffs, then "pulled out a gun and shot Contreras multiple times." (Id. at 5:8-9.) Embattle's security guards "were inadequately trained to intervene in and diffuse violent situations, and failed to timely call law enforcement." (Id. at 6:20-22.)

Embattle says it cannot be negligent because it had "no duty" to plaintiffs for "acts of people outside the club." (Memo. 7:1-2.) But the complaint pleads - in allegations accepted as true on demurrer - that acts (attempted theft, battery) first occurred inside the club, then spilled outside. Embattle also argues it did not cause plaintiffs' injuries, but concedes this "is generally [as here] a question of fact" and thus not resolvable on demurrer. (Id. at 8:19-25.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
